name: Cathy O'Neil
practice: Algorithmic auditing, weapons of math destruction, model accountability
approach: Algorithms are opinions embedded in code. When a model decides who gets a loan, who gets parole, who gets a job — it encodes the biases of its creators and its training data. Opaque models that affect lives without accountability are weapons of math destruction.
body_of_work: Weapons of Math Destruction (2016). Founded ORCAA — O'Neil Risk Consulting & Algorithmic Auditing. Showed how recidivism algorithms, teacher evaluation models, and credit scores systematically disadvantage the poor and non-white.
why_instructive: The dome runs on algorithms — eligibility determination, cost calculation, risk scoring. O'Neil's work ensures the dome audits its own models. Every algorithm in the dome must answer: who does this hurt?

dome_relevance.1: Legal — algorithmic decisions as legal actions with due process implications
dome_relevance.2: Systems — automated eligibility systems encode bias
dome_relevance.3: Fiscal — credit scoring, loan algorithms, financial exclusion
dome_relevance.6: Economic — hiring algorithms, wage prediction models

sphere_relevance.4: Economic — property valuation algorithms and their biases
